raw_response:
The earliest transient changes post-tissue injury would likely involve an inflammatory response leading to localized cellular migration and recruitment into the affected area. This process involves primarily neutrophils initially moving towards the site of damage to initiate innate immunity against potential pathogens before subsequent involvement of other cell types. Given these considerations, the earliest transient change following tissue injury is expected to reflect neutrophil activity, aligning with option **\boxed{A}**.
This reasoning points directly to the immediate influx of neutrophils as one of the first responses to tissue injury, making them central in initiating the body’s defense mechanism. Thus, among given choices, neutrophilia (\(\boxed{\text{A}}\)) represents the earliest physiological marker reflecting activation upon tissue injury. Other changes noted might develop subsequently.
Thus, I select \(\boxed{\text{A}}\) considering both biological sequence and available evidence supporting earlier neutrophil mobilization compared to late markers like monocytosis or prolonged lymphocytosis.
